title: Total War: ROME II - Emperor Edition
notes: Launcher starts fine, when the you click play the fame refuses to launch.

title: Total War: ROME II - Emperor Edition
notes:
At launch, "Performing first time setup... Installing: VCRedist 2008 (Step 2 of 8)". Error box popup, Title "Microsoft Visual C++ 2005", Message "Error creating process <msiexec /i vcredist.msi /qn /promptrestart>. Reason: File not found." 

Launcher will open after, but attempting to run the game will crash.

title: Total War: ROME II - Emperor Edition
notes: Won't Start

title: Total War: Rome II - Emperor Edition
notes: Some terrain/shadow glitches, benchmark ~30fps with 'extreme' settings

title: Total War: ROME II - Emperor Edition
notes: Performance and stability fine. Crashes when starting manual battle from campaign map. The Issue seems to be DXVK-related since it does not occur when running the game on Wine without DXVK.

title: Total War: ROME II - Emperor Edition
notes: Generally good framerate (~120 fps on benchmark with Ultra preset). Has battery life indicator visible in top center of screen that should have a toggle in Advanced Graphic Options but doesn't. No crashes yet.

title: Total War™: ROME II - Emperor Edition
notes: As of Sept 5th 2018 Rome II now seems to work after an update.  If running mods, you may need to edit the preferences.script.txt file to launch the game in windowd mode in order to avoid a black screen at launch. Then go into options and set fullscreen from there.

title: Total War™: ROME II - Emperor Edition
notes: Performance and stability fine. Crashes when loading battles on elaborate maps (esp. with many trees). The issue seems to be DXVK-related since it does not occur when running the game on Wine without DXVK. As a workaround lowering the graphics settings prevents these crashes.

title: Total War™: ROME II - Emperor Edition

notes: When you minimize you get a pretty blank screen. The only solution is open system monitor and kill the process. The game is playable with a good frame rate. Just don't minimize it!!
title: Total War™: ROME II - Emperor Edition

notes: If you use very high graphic setting you can't enter to battle mod. With lower graphic settings it's work.
title: Total War™: ROME II - Emperor Edition

notes: The launcher for me that lists  your total war games is just a blank white box. But if you know where the big Play button is you can click in that area and the game starts and works perfectly.
title: Total War™: ROME II - Emperor Edition

gpu: X.Org Radeon RX Vega (VEGA10, DRM 3.26.0, 4.18.10-arch1-1-ARCH, LLVM 6.0.1)
notes: Performance wise the game runs great. I only noticed a few minor graphical issues such as a dirt texture which gets pushed in to the camera when you get too close to the ground in certain locations but nothing game breaking.
title: Total War™: ROME II - Emperor Edition

notes:
Game runs fine but takes an incredible amount of time to load. 10-15 minutes to get past the loading screen. I cannot find anyone else with this issue however so i appear to be the only one.
Test with 410.66 and 396.54.09 drivers
title: Total War™: ROME II - Emperor Edition

notes: Launcher is a white box. Integrated benchmark crashes. Runs fine otherwise.
title: Total War™: ROME II - Emperor Edition

notes: Works very well, but had to lower graphics settings as proposed by other users, otherwise was crashing to desktop when going to battle map
title: Total War™: ROME II - Emperor Edition

notes: Initially I could not figure out how to play the game since the launcher was just a big white box. After reading reviews here I realized that you can guess where the big red "Play" button is located. I did a simple google search for "rome total war 2 launcher" to get an idea of where the button is. After pressing on the correct area of the white box, I can launch the game. Afterwards, everything runs smoothly.

title: Total War™: ROME II - Emperor Edition
Total War™: ROME II - Emperor Edition

notes: Launcher is just a white box, but the "play" button position can be guessed. Clicking in play button start the game. Manually setting to direct launching the game executable also works. In game, single player campaign works well, but not tested with multiplayer. Benchmark crashes the game.
title: Total War™: ROME II - Emperor Edition

notes: Installed Rome and played. The audio is a bit laggy sometimes and I sometimes I would have a crash here and then(after some hours of play) but other than that it worked. Also I would mention that I could not play naval battles
title: Total War™: ROME II - Emperor Edition

notes: Google the launcher to get an approximation of where to click is the launcher is blank, and change the shader settings to 4.1 in order to stop the game crashing when entering battles
title: Total War™: ROME II - Emperor Edition

notes: The launcher is all white but functional when clicking the right place. Crashes infrequently when playing battles. Always crashes when playing naval battles in the campaign.
title: Total War™: ROME II - Emperor Edition

notes: White launcher, game plays almost perfectly, only issues are graphics settings only works up to 'high' and sound is often distorted.
title: Total War™: ROME II - Emperor Edition

notes:
As noted by others, launcher is blank white, which can be overcome, however, if you want to use Workshop mods, you are SOL. You can still manually install mods and then add the line "" mod [modname] ; "" (note semicolon) to your "" preferences.script.txt "" file in "" /steamapps/compatdata/214950/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Application Data/The Creative Assembly/Rome2/scripts "", and be sure to set the first line of that file to false to avoid it overwriting your changes each time you play.

Unfortunately, most Workshop mods are unavailable outside the workshop, and while a few do download as .pack files, which can be copy/pasted into the game's data folder and used without the launcher, most are .bin files and I don't know how to get them to work. TWC forums have a lot of the big mods available (like DEI, which works just fine), so this may or may not be an issue for you. Smaller mods, tweaks, etc, are hard to get working.

On to the game itself, performance is GREAT, however there are battlemap crashes with certain graphics settings, though you can max out a lot of them without issue. Anything that just increases models/textures is fine to go to extreme, but stuff that impacts rendering/postprocess/etc might be more challenging, like shadows, and others have already mentioned the rendering process used.
title: Total War™: ROME II - Emperor Edition

notes: [added to previous comment] you CAN get higher settings than "High", but with a hitch: they need to be reset between battles, AND they can't all be set at once. So you can push most settings to extreme (excluding water/shadows/DoF), but try only doing a few at a time, accepting, and doing the next few. Doing it all at once causes a crash. Also, even if you manage to get them all working for one battle, if you load up the next battle without reverting to the High setting, you'll crash, because again it's all being loaded up at once. I think you will want to check "unlimited video memory" first thing as well. But I've gotten the game to basically max graphical settings, with a few Ultras, and with otherwise flawless performance, but it takes some patience. I'll note there's also sometimes issues with the loader, in that you can click all day and have it not load. You need to close it out (top right corner) and try again, sometimes using your process explorer to kill any launcher.exe or wine.exe programs running. It usually works fine the first time you start in a session, but subsequent times will be more problematic.
title: Total War™: ROME II - Emperor Edition

notes:
Opt into the launcher beta as it is much more compatible running in Linux. 

To remove the battery icon on a desktop: show hidden files and then navigate to .steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/214950/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Application Data/The Creative Assembly/Rome2/scripts 

Open preferences.script.txt and set "batter_meter false" in line 55.

There seems to be an issue with mods at the moment. To use mods, first open the game and go to Options->Mods and then enable user script mods.

From there go to the previous mentioned directory and create a file called user.script.txt and then add your mods using the following syntax:
mod "your mod name.pack"; Add a new line for each mod.

Save and then load the game.

You can find these mod names by navigating to .steam/steam/steamapps/common/Total War Rome II/data and copying the name of your mod packs there.
